question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: A bug in the respective layer 0/1/2 network code that results in unintended smart contract behavior with no concrete funds at direct risk'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the asymmetry between rateLimitBucket (no sync) and gasLimitBucket (SYNCHRONIZED) to craft a timing attack where the rate limit check passes in a race but the synchronized gas check serializes execution, causing inconsistent throttle enforcement and unintended smart contract execution ordering?
